acceptedAnswer.text: No. But an unregistered firm cannot sue third parties, and partners cannot sue each other. Registration provides legal protection and is required for opening bank accounts in some states.

mainEntity.name: How many partners can a firm have?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minimum 2. Maximum 50 partners (Companies Act, 2013, Section 464). For banking: maximum 10.

mainEntity.name: What is the cost of partnership registration?

acceptedAnswer.text: Government fee: varies by state (₹500-₹2,000). Professional fee: ₹5,000-₹8,000 including partnership deed drafting. Stamp duty on deed varies by state. Timeline: 7-15 days.

mainEntity.name: What is the liability of partners?

acceptedAnswer.text: Unlimited liability. Each partner is personally liable for all debts of the firm. Joint and several liability — creditors can pursue any one partner for full amount. This is the key disadvantage vs LLP or Pvt Ltd.

mainEntity.name: How many partners can a partnership have?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minimum: 2. Maximum: 50 for any partnership (Banking Regulation Act limits to 10 for banking business). For more than 50 persons: must register as company or LLP.

mainEntity.name: Can a partnership be converted to LLP?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Under Section 56 of LLP Act. All partners become partners in LLP. Partnership dissolved. No stamp duty on conversion (in most states). No capital gains tax implications if conditions met.
